page_content:
BSCI223 General Microbiology (4 Credits)

How have microorganisms exploited every conceivable ecological

niche, and how have humans harnessed this diversity to improve medicine, agriculture, and biotechnology? The course covers fundamental concepts in morphology, physiology, genetics, immunology, ecology, and pathogenic microbiology. Applications of microbiology to medicine, the food industry, and biotechnology.
